name: Systematic Debugging
description: Bisect the problem space systematically — reproduce, hypothesize, bisect, repeat — instead of guessing.

name: Incremental Development
description: Build and ship the smallest working increment, then iterate — avoid big-bang rewrites.

name: Defensive Interface Design
description: Design APIs and interfaces to make misuse hard and correct use obvious.

name: Test-Driven Red-Green-Refactor
description: Write a failing test first, make it pass with minimal code, then refactor — every production line is justified by a test.

name: Contract-First API Design
description: Define the full API contract as an OpenAPI spec before any implementation so producers and consumers build in parallel.

name: Measure Before Optimize
description: Profile first to locate the actual bottleneck — never optimize by intuition or assumption.

name: Security Threat Modeling
description: Apply STRIDE to enumerate trust boundaries and attack surfaces at design time, then encode mitigations as tests.

name: Checklist-Driven Code Review
description: Review in layered order — tests, architecture, correctness, security, performance, style — at 200–400 lines per hour.

name: Read Before Write
description: Read the full function/class, check callers and tests before modifying existing code — the #1 defense against AI-generated bugs.

name: Error Handling Patterns
description: When to catch vs propagate, specific vs broad exceptions, logging on catch — never swallow errors silently.

name: Git Workflow for Agents
description: Commit discipline, branch strategy, and safety rules for an AI agent writing code in git-tracked repos.

name: Codebase Navigation
description: Efficiently map an unfamiliar codebase — find entry points, trace call chains, grep strategies, read tests first.

name: Safe Refactoring
description: Extract, rename, move with a test safety net — never refactor and change behavior in the same commit.

name: Dependency Management
description: When to add a dep vs write it yourself, pin versions, audit for vulnerabilities, upgrade safely.

name: Logging and Observability
description: What to log (boundaries, decisions, errors), structured logging, log levels, and never log secrets.

name: AI Code Generation Quality
description: Self-check for hallucinated APIs, outdated patterns, missing edge cases, and over-engineering in AI-generated code.

name: Bug Pattern Detection
description: Scan code for common bug patterns: concurrency issues, error handling gaps, resource leaks, logic errors, security holes.

name: Root Cause Analysis
description: Five Whys method for finding the real cause of bugs — not just what broke, but why, and how to prevent recurrence.
